title: Integrating Large Language Model-Based Agents into a Virtual Patient Chatbot for Clinical Anamnesis Training
citation: Laverde N, Grevisse C, Jaramillo S, Manrique R. Integrating large language model-based agents into a virtual patient chatbot for clinical anamnesis training. Computational and Structural Biotechnology Journal. 2025;27:2481-2491. doi:10.1016/j.csbj.2025.05.025.
permitted_uses: Architecture inspiration for virtual patient memory, retrieval, reflection, planning, case templates, usability evaluation, and response-grounding QA.
claims_supported: The paper describes a generative-agent architecture with memory stream, retriever, cognitive mechanisms, and interview functionality.
The retriever combines recency, importance, and relevance metrics and reports k=4 as satisfactory in the implementation.
The implementation used predefined cases for diabetes, dyslipidemia, and joint pain, plus a teacher-created custom case workflow.
The system categorized responses as explicit, implicit, or fictional relative to case descriptions and assessed plausibility with specialists.
The study reported a CUQ score of 86.25 out of 100 and identified latency, voice realism, hallucination, bias, and limited objective skill-improvement evidence as limitations.
claims_not_supported: That OpenClinXR's XR multi-actor stations are validated.
That LLM-generated responses can be used for high-stakes scoring.
That avatar video generation should run synchronously during a Quest 3 WebXR station.
